architecture.summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are trained on the residual stream activations of ESM-2, a protein language model. The SAE decomposes pLM hidden states into interpretable sparse features. Features are characterized through linear probing onto protein properties (thermostability, subcellular localization) and visualized to identify sequence determinants and hypothesize unknown biological mechanisms.
trained_components: sparse autoencoder (SAE) on ESM-2 residual stream

biblio.title: From Mechanistic Interpretability to Mechanistic Biology: Training, Evaluating, and Interpreting Sparse Autoencoders on Protein Language Models

claims.statement: pLMs use a combination of generic features and family-specific features to represent a protein
Known sequence determinants of properties such as thermostability and subcellular localization can be identified by linear probing of SAE features
For predictive features without known functional associations, the study hypothesizes their role in unknown mechanisms and provides visualization tools to aid their interpretation
